economic_notes: Band Revenue segment includes campsite operations, property taxation, and resource management. Subsequent to year-end, the First Nation acquired four parcels of land for $5,200,000 paid in cash. Nation received additional COVID-19 government transfers.
notes: Audit is qualified due to missing budget comparison information (not a scope limitation on financial data). Prior year (2020) included a $21,428,571 specific claim settlement which inflated Band Revenue expenses (salaries $9.5M in 2020 vs $590K in 2021) and revenue significantly. Four subsidiaries consolidated: Chowethel Axwest Managing Partner Ltd., Chowethel Qaqelstexw LLP, Chowethel Projects LP, Chowethel Projects GP Ltd. Subsequent event: $5.2M land purchase post year-end. Total government transfers calculated as ISC ($5,136,318) + Other funding ($686,819) + Province of BC ($444,730) + CMHC ($46,402) + BC FN Gaming Revenue Sharing LP ($414,930) = $6,729,199; OSR = rental income ($311,141) + economic activity and other ($1,514,495) = $1,825,636. Note: revenue lines labeled Other funding and BC FN Gaming Revenue Sharing LP and Province of BC treated as government transfers. OSR recalculated as economic activity and other plus rental income.
warnings: Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
